title: Health impacts of microplastic and nanoplastic exposure
locator: Abstract; sections on analytical challenges, human evidence and risk assessment
relevance: Separates human detection and early association evidence from causal or population-level risk conclusions.
limitations: A narrative review; it does not itself validate a clinical assay or establish causality.

title: Monitoring microplastics in drinking water: An interlaboratory study to inform effective methods for quantifying and characterizing microplastics
locator: Abstract; 22-laboratory study design and recovery, blank-contamination and spectroscopy results
relevance: Shows both strong performance above a stated size threshold and substantial contamination and workflow dependence.
limitations: Simulated clean-water samples do not establish performance in tissue, blood or complex environmental matrices.

title: Interlaboratory Comparison Reveals State of the Art in Microplastic Detection and Quantification Methods
locator: Abstract; 84-laboratory VAMAS comparison, materials, methods and reproducibility ranges
relevance: Tests two thermo-analytical and three spectroscopic approaches on common reference materials and exposes remaining reproducibility limits.
limitations: The water-soluble tablet matrix and 10–200 μm PE/PET materials do not represent all polymers, sizes or biological matrices.

title: Detection of microplastics in human lung tissue using μFTIR spectroscopy
locator: Abstract; n=13 tissue study, 3 μm method limit, blank controls and adjusted results
relevance: Provides a contamination-controlled example of reported microplastics in human lung tissue.
limitations: Small observational tissue series; detection supports an exposure route, not disease causation or population prevalence.

title: Microplastics and Nanoplastics in Atheromas and Cardiovascular Events
locator: Abstract; Methods, Results and Conclusions; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05900947
relevance: Reports polymer detection in excised carotid plaques and a prospective association with a composite cardiovascular endpoint.
limitations: Observational study in a selected surgical population; the association does not establish that detected particles caused the events.

title: Bioaccumulation of microplastics in decedent human brains
locator: Abstract; complementary-method confirmation and postmortem cohort comparisons
relevance: Reports micro- and nanoplastic signals in decedent kidney, liver and brain using complementary methods.
limitations: Postmortem observational data; between-cohort differences do not establish exposure history, disease causation, living burden or clinical actionability.

title: Effects of Microplastic Exposure on Human Digestive, Reproductive, and Respiratory Health: A Rapid Systematic Review
locator: Abstract; eligibility, included-study counts and outcome-specific evidence ratings
relevance: Offers a structured appraisal that reaches suspected-harm conclusions for selected outcomes using human and animal evidence.
limitations: Only three included human observational studies covered prioritized outcomes; many conclusions depend materially on animal evidence and do not establish human clinical causality.

title: Are microplastics bad for your health? More rigorous science is needed
authors_or_organization: Xu J-L, Wright S, Rauert C, Thomas KV
locator: Full commentary; methodological critique and recommendations
relevance: Documents expert concern that stronger analytical quality and study design are needed before broad health conclusions.
limitations: Commentary, not a new experimental dataset or formal systematic review.

title: Microplastics Research
locator: Overview; Research Efforts > Methods Development and Standardization
relevance: States the federal research need for standardized collection, extraction, quantification and identification methods.
limitations: Program overview; it is not a regulatory limit, validated clinical method or comparative performance study.

title: Advancing Micro- and Nanoplastic (MNP) Metrology
authors_or_organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
locator: Reference Materials; Separations; Emerging Measurement Methods; updated 24 November 2025
relevance: Defines measurement outputs and describes reference-material, separation and emerging-instrument work.
limitations: Research roadmap and capability descriptions; not certification that all listed methods are routine or cross-matrix validated.

title: ISO 24187:2023 — Principles for the analysis of microplastics present in the environment
locator: Abstract; publication and scope fields
relevance: Confirms an international principles-and-minimum-requirements standard for environmental matrices.
limitations: It does not prescribe monitoring requirements and does not validate human biomonitoring or clinical interpretation.

title: Microplastics Drinking Water
authors_or_organization: California State Water Resources Control Board
locator: Actions; Testing and Reporting; Resolution 2022-0032 and linked policy handbook
relevance: Documents a standard method, laboratory-accreditation work and a four-year monitoring/reporting framework for drinking water.
limitations: Drinking-water policy does not establish a health threshold or validate methods for blood and tissue.

title: EPA Takes Bold Action to Ensure Drinking Water is Safe from Microplastics, Pharmaceuticals, and Forever Chemicals
locator: Draft CCL 6 announcement; candidate-list status and next steps
relevance: Confirms that microplastics entered draft CCL 6 as a research and prioritization signal.
limitations: Candidate listing is not a drinking-water limit, compliance rule or finding of population-level health risk.

title: Commission Regulation (EU) 2023/2055 — restriction of microplastics intentionally added to products
locator: Scope; Exclusions; Transition periods; guidance update dated 18 July 2025
relevance: Shows a concrete product-compliance trigger for intentionally added synthetic polymer microparticles.
limitations: The restriction has defined scope, exclusions and transition periods; it is not a blanket ban on all plastics or all microplastic emissions.

title: Synthetic Trace Observation and Medical Products (STOMP)
authors_or_organization: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health
locator: The Problem; The Solution; Technical Areas; Program Timeline
relevance: Defines the program's staged measurement, mechanism and later removal agenda.
limitations: A program roadmap and solicitation description do not establish that the sought technologies already work.

title: STOMP Frequently Asked Questions
authors_or_organization: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health
locator: Funding; Technical Areas; TA3 timing; validation and reference-material answers; Amendment 2
relevance: Clarifies that $144 million is a program commitment across anticipated awards and that removal TA3 is not in the initial solicitation.
limitations: Anticipated program structure is not evidence of awards, successful performers or technical validation.

title: ARPA-H launches groundbreaking program to combat toxic microplastics in the body
authors_or_organization: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health
locator: Program launch; $144 million commitment; two-phase structure and independent validation
relevance: Provides the announced program envelope and intended federal validation role.
limitations: Launch language is directional; the FAQ and solicitation govern timing and distinguish commitment from awards.

title: Exposomics
authors_or_organization: National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences
locator: Definition; Research Priority Areas; data standards, harmonization and human exposome atlas feasibility
relevance: Places particle exposure in a broader lifetime-exposure discipline and identifies harmonization as infrastructure.
limitations: A research strategy, not a market forecast or proof that a unified exposome platform is clinically actionable.

title: Dietary and inhalation exposure to nano- and microplastic particles and potential implications for human health
locator: Overview; evidence reviewed through December 2021; research needs and uncertainties
relevance: Provides a cautious cross-route health evidence baseline and explicitly identifies research gaps.
limitations: Its evidence cutoff is December 2021, so it cannot adjudicate later tissue, cardiovascular or intervention studies.

title: Can Plasma Exchange Be Used to Lower the Circulating Burden of Microplastics in Human Patients?
locator: Methods; Results Table 2; Discussion and study limitations; conflict-of-interest statement
relevance: Reports baseline-stratified changes in a proprietary circulating-particle assay across 174 plasma-exchange procedures in 114 patients.
limitations: No randomized control, tissue or total-body measurement, durability or health outcome; assay validation is proprietary, particles below 1 μm are excluded, and tubing/replacement fluid may add particles at lower baselines.

title: Evaluating DFPP for Microplastic Reduction (DFPP-MNP), NCT07658443
locator: Study Overview; Design; Outcome Measures; Enrollment; Record Dates
relevance: Shows a prospective paired-biomarker pilot intended to test circulating-particle change after clinically prescribed DFPP.
limitations: Estimated enrollment is 20, no results are posted, treatment is not assigned by the study, and the primary endpoint is a biomarker rather than clinical benefit.

title: Drinking Water Contaminant Candidate List 6 — Draft
locator: 91 FR 17186; Microplastics, printed pages 17193–17194; four stated research needs
relevance: Identifies microplastics as a draft drinking-water research priority and states the method and risk-definition gaps.
limitations: The draft candidate list imposes no requirement, creates no monitoring mandate and establishes no health-based limit.

title: Revisions To Establish the Sixth Unregulated Contaminant Monitoring Rule (UCMR 6) for Public Water Systems
locator: 91 FR 39952; section III.E.1 Microplastics, printed pages 39959–39960
relevance: Explains why microplastics are absent from proposed UCMR 6 and what must change for future national monitoring.
limitations: UCMR 6 is proposed, not final; omission from this cycle is not a conclusion that microplastics are safe or irrelevant.

title: Potential Health Impact of Microplastics: A Review of Environmental Distribution, Human Exposure, and Toxic Effects
locator: Introduction; environmental distribution (Antarctic to Arctic, Mount Everest to the Mariana Trench)
relevance: Documents the ubiquitous environmental distribution of microplastics and the primary human exposure pathways.
limitations: A review establishing environmental presence and exposure pathways; it does not establish population-level disease or causation.
